  • The first command word causes the gem to shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. This effect doesn’t expend a charge. It lasts until you use a bonus action to repeat the command word or until you use another function of the gem.

  • The second command word expends 1 charge and causes the gem to fire a brilliant beam of light at one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a [[/save 15 CON]] or become blinded for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

  • The third command word expends 5 charges and causes the gem to flare with blinding light in a 30-foot cone originating from it. Each creature in the cone must succeed on a [[/save 15 CON]] or become blinded for 1 minute.

When all of the gem’s charges are expended, the gem becomes a nonmagical jewel worth 50 gp.

Bright Light.name: Bright Light
Bright Light.description: The first command word causes the gem to shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. This effect doesn’t expend a charge. It lasts until you use a bonus action to repeat the command word or until you use another function of the gem.

Brilliant Beam.name: Brilliant Beam
Brilliant Beam.description: The second command word expends 1 charge and causes the gem to fire a brilliant beam of light at one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a [[/save DC 15 CON]] save or become &Reference[blinded] for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Flare of Light.name: Flare of Light
Flare of Light.description: The third command word expends 5 charges and causes the gem to flare with blinding light in a 30-foot cone originating from it. Each creature in the cone must succeed on a [[/save DC 15 CON]] save or become blinded for 1 minute.

Gem of Seeing.name: Gem of Seeing
Gem of Seeing.description: This gem has 3 charges. As an action, you can speak the gem’s command word and expend 1 charge. For the next 10 minutes, you have truesight to a range of 120 feet when you peer through the gem. The gem regains [[/r 1d3]] expended charges daily at dawn.

Giant Slayer.name: Giant Slayer
Giant Slayer.description:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you use it to hit a creature with the Giant type, the creature takes an extra [[/r 2d6]] damage of the weapon’s type and must succeed on a [[/save 15 STR]] or fall prone.

Foundry Note

Weapon (Any Axe or Sword), Rare, 1,500 gp + base item cost

Create the final magic item by selection the appropriate mundane item and dragging an enchantment from the Advancement & Effects tab to that item.

Giant Save.name: Giant Save
Giant Save.condition: When you hit a creature with the Giant type
Giant Save.activationCondition: When you hit a creature with the Giant type

Gloves of Missile Snaring.name: Gloves of Missile Snaring
Gloves of Missile Snaring.description: These gloves seem to almost meld into your hands when you don them. When a ranged weapon attack hits you while you’re wearing them, and you have a free hand, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage by [[/r 1d10]] + your DEX modifier. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can catch the missile if it is small enough for you to hold in that hand.

Catch Missile.name: Catch Missile
Catch Missile.condition: When a ranged weapon attack hits you
Catch Missile.activationCondition: When a ranged weapon attack hits you

Gloves of Swimming and Climbing.name: Gloves of Swimming and Climbing
Gloves of Swimming and Climbing.description: While wearing these gloves, climbing and swimming don’t cost you extra movement, and you gain a +5 bonus to STR (Athletics) checks made to climb or swim.

Goggles of Night.name: Goggles of Night
Goggles of Night.description: While wearing these goggles, you have darkvision to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, wearing the goggles increases its range by 60 feet.

Gray Bag of Tricks.name: Gray Bag of Tricks
Gray Bag of Tricks.description:
This ordinary bag, made from gray cloth, appears empty. Reaching inside the bag, however, reveals a small, fuzzy object. The bag weighs 1/2 pound.

You can use an action to pull the fuzzy object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When the object lands, it transforms into a creature you determine by rolling a d8 and consulting the table that corresponds to the bag’s color. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it is reduced to 0 HP.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and it acts on your turn. You can use a bonus action to command how the creature moves and what action it takes on its next turn, or to give it general orders, such as to attack your enemies. In the absence of such orders, the creature acts in a fashion appropriate to its nature.

Once three fuzzy objects have been pulled from the bag, the bag can’t be used again until the next dawn.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.PBo9j135EucIDliX.JournalEntryPage.MaHQIrVso0HQQS1f inline]

Half Plate.name: Half Plate
Half Plate.description: Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It doesn’t include leg protection beyond greaves attached with leather straps.

Handy Haversack.name: Handy Haversack
Handy Haversack.description:
This backpack has a central pouch and two side pouches, each of which is an extradimensional space. Each side pouch can hold up to 20 pounds of material, not exceeding a volume of 2 cubic feet. The large central pouch can hold up to 8 cubic feet or 80 pounds of material. The backpack always weighs 5 pounds, regardless of its contents.

Placing an object in the haversack follows the normal rules for interacting with objects. Retrieving an item from the haversack requires you to use an action. When you reach into the haversack for a specific item, the item is always magically on top.

The haversack has a few limitations. If it is overloaded, or if a sharp object pierces it or tears it, the haversack ruptures and is destroyed. If the haversack is destroyed, its contents are lost forever, although exceptionally powerful magic items lost in the bag always turn up again somewhere. If the haversack is turned inside out, its contents spill forth, unharmed, and the haversack must be put right before it can be used again. If a breathing creature is placed within the haversack, the creature can survive for up to 10 minutes, after which time it begins to suffocate.

Placing the haversack inside an extradimensional space created by a bag of holding, portable hole, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate is sucked through it and deposited in a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can’t be reopened.

Hat of Disguise.name: Hat of Disguise
Hat of Disguise.description: While wearing this hat, you can use an action to cast the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.75HF18ljbTk7wZwC]{disguise self} spell from it. The spell ends if the hat is removed.

Headband of Intellect.name: Headband of Intellect
Headband of Intellect.description: Your INT score is 19 while you wear this headband. It has no effect on you if your INT is already 19 or higher.

Healer's Kit.name: Healer's Kit
Healer's Kit.description: This kit is a leather pouch containing bandages, salves, and splints. The kit has 10 uses. As an action, you can expend one use of the kit to stabilize a creature that has 0 HP without needing to make a WIS (Medicine) check.

Stabilize.name: Stabilize
Stabilize.description: You stabilize a creature that has 0 hit points without needing to make a WIS (Medicine) check.

Helm of Brilliance.name: Helm of Brilliance
Helm of Brilliance.description:
This dazzling helm is set with [[/r 1d10]] diamonds, [[/r 2d10]] rubies, [[/r 3d10]] fire opals, and [[/r 4d10]] opals. Any gem pried from the helm crumbles to dust. When all the gems are removed or destroyed, the helm loses its magic.

You gain the following benefits while wearing it:

  • You can use an action to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18), using one of the helm’s gems of the specified type as a component: daylight (opal), fireball (fire opal), prismatic spray (diamond), or wall of fire (ruby). The gem is destroyed when the spell is cast and disappears from the helm.

  • As long as it has at least one diamond, the helm emits dim light in a 30-foot radius when at least one Undead is within that area. Any Undead that starts its turn in that area takes [[/damage 1d6 radiant]].

  • As long as the helm has at least one ruby, you have resistance to fire damage.

  • As long as the helm has at least one fire opal, you can use an action and speak a command word to cause one weapon you are wielding to burst into flames. The flames emit bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. The flames are harmless to you and the weapon. When you hit with an attack using the blazing weapon, the target takes an extra [[/damage 1d6 fire]]. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or stow the weapon.

Roll a d20 if you are wearing the helm and take fire damage as a result of failing a save against a spell. On a roll of 1, the helm emits beams of light from its remaining gems. Each creature within 60 feet of the helm other than you must succeed on a [[/save 17 DEX]] or be struck by a beam, taking radiant damage equal to the number of gems in the helm. The helm and its gems are then destroyed.

Ignite Weapon.name: Ignite Weapon
Ignite Weapon.condition: As long as the helm has at least one fire opal
Ignite Weapon.activationCondition: As long as the helm has at least one fire opal

Helm of Brilliance.name: Helm of Brilliance
Helm of Brilliance.description: As long as the helm has at least one ruby, you have resistance to fire damage.

Helm of Comprehending Languages.name: Helm of Comprehending Languages
Helm of Comprehending Languages.description: While wearing this helm, you can use an action to cast the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.wyKjdoHh1PVEId7q]{comprehend languages} spell from it.

Helm of Telepathy.name: Helm of Telepathy
Helm of Telepathy.description:
While wearing this helm, you can use an action to cast the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.q3ggx5aH69iqrcL3]{detect thoughts} spell (save DC 13) from it. As long as you maintain concentration on the spell, you can use a bonus action to send a telepathic message to a creature you are focused on. It can reply—using a bonus action to do so—while your focus on it continues.

While focusing on a creature with detect thoughts, you can use an action to cast the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.iw5BfQdWrDssHFWZ]{suggestion} spell (save DC 13) from the helm on that creature. You don’t need to concentrate to maintain suggestion for the duration of the spell, but it ends if the detect thoughts spell ends. Once used, the suggestion property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

cast.condition: When focusing on a creature with detect thoughts
cast.activationCondition: When focusing on a creature with detect thoughts

Helm of Teleportation.name: Helm of Teleportation
Helm of Teleportation.description: This helm has 3 charges. While wearing it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.NJKjBBmBG98Lo2XY]{teleport} spell from it. The helm regains [[/r 1d3]] expended charges daily at dawn.

Herbalist Tools.name: Herbalist Tools
Herbalist Tools.description:
Herbalist tools are used to store and brew plants and other organic materials into potions, antidotes, and poisons.

Associated Abilities: INT, WIS.

Components. This tool kit includes glass vials, a mortar and pestle, pouches for ingredient storage, clippers, a pair of leather gloves, and a small pot for brewing.

Craft Items. Herbalist tools can be used to make items like those listed in the Herbal Concoctions section of the Adventuring Gear table, with the crafting downtime activity.

Hide.name: Hide
Hide.description: This full-body suit of armor consists of thick furs and pelts.

Holy Avenger.name: Holy Avenger
Holy Avenger.description:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a Fiend or an Undead with it, the target takes an extra [[/damage 2d10 radiant]].

While you wield this sword, it creates an aura in a 10-foot radius around you. You and all creatures friendly to you in the aura have advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects. If you have 17 or more levels in the paladin class, the radius of the aura increases to 30 feet.

Foundry Note

Weapon (Any Sword), Legendary, 200,000 gp (Requires Attunement)

Create the final magic item by selection the appropriate mundane item and dragging an enchantment from the Advancement & Effects tab to that item.

Holy Symbol.name: Holy Symbol
Holy Symbol.description: This item can be used as a spellcasting focus to channel Divine spells. Examples include an amulet depicting a deity’s symbol, that same symbol engraved or inlaid as an emblem on a shield, or a tiny box holding a fragment of a sacred relic.

Holy Water (flask).name: Holy Water (flask)
Holy Water (flask).description:
As an action, you can splash the contents of this flask onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw it up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. In either case, make a ranged attack against a target creature, treating the holy water as an improvised weapon. If the target is a fiend or undead, it takes [[/damage 2d6 radiant]] damage—otherwise, the water has no effect.

A cleric may create holy water by performing a special ritual. The ritual takes 1 hour to perform, uses 25 gp worth of powdered silver, and requires the caster to expend a 1st-ring spell slot.

Hooded Lantern.name: Hooded Lantern
Hooded Lantern.description: A hooded lantern casts bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Once lit, it burns for 6 hours on a flask (1 pint) of oil. As an action, you can lower the hood, reducing the light to dim light in a 5-foot radius.

Horn.name: Horn
Horn.description:
Musical instruments come in many shapes and sizes with varying regional and cultural popularity. When you have proficiency in a musical instrument, choose one type: strings, brass, woodwinds, percussion, or keyboard instruments. You must have multiple proficiencies to have proficiency with more than one type of instrument.

Associated Abilities: DEX, CHA.

Components. A musical instrument typically includes an instrument, a protective case, and any small pieces necessary for play, such as picks or reeds.

Horn of Blasting.name: Horn of Blasting
Horn of Blasting.description:
You can use an action to speak the horn’s command word and then blow the horn, which emits a thunderous blast in a 30-foot cone that is audible to 600 feet. Each creature in the cone must make a [[/save 15 CON]]. On a failed save, a creature takes [[/damage 5d6 thunder]] and is &Reference[deafened] for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn’t deafened. Creatures and objects made of glass or crystal have disadvantage on the saves and take [[/damage 10d6 thunder]] instead of [[/r 5d6]].

Each use of the horn’s magic has a 20 percent chance of causing the horn to explode. The explosion deals [[/damage 10d6 fire]] to the blower and destroys the horn.

Horseshoes of a Zephyr.name: Horseshoes of a Zephyr
Horseshoes of a Zephyr.description: These iron horseshoes come in a set of four. While all four shoes are affixed to the hooves of a horse or similar creature, they allow the creature to move normally while floating 4 inches above the ground. This effect means the creature can cross or stand above nonsolid or unstable surfaces, such as water or lava. The creature leaves no tracks and ignores difficult terrain. In addition, the creature can move at normal speed for up to 12 hours a day without suffering exhaustion from a forced march.

Zephyr.name: Zephyr
Zephyr.description: The creature leaves no tracks and ignores difficult terrain.

Howling Arrows.name: Howling Arrows
Howling Arrows.description:
These arrowheads are made from hollowed antler and emit a shrill whistle while airborne. A successful attack made with a howling arrow deals only half damage, but the target must succeed on a [[/save 11 CON]] or be deafened until the end of its next turn.

Foundry Note

Wondrous Item (Any Piercing Ammunition), Common, 10 gp

Create the final magic item by selection the appropriate mundane item and dragging an enchantment from the Advancement & Effects tab to that item.

Hunting Trap (basic).name: Hunting Trap (basic)
Hunting Trap (basic).description: As an action, you can set a trap in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. When set, this trap forms a saw-toothed steel ring that snaps shut when a creature steps on a pressure plate in the center. A Large or smaller creature that enters the trap’s space must succeed on a [[/save dexterity 13]] save or take [[/damage 1d4 piercing]] damage and become grappled until freed. A creature can use its action to make a [[/check strength athletics 13]] check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Each failed check deals 1 piercing damage to the trapped creature.

Trigger Trap.name: Trigger Trap
Trigger Trap.condition: When a Large or smaller creature that enters the trap’s space
Trigger Trap.activationCondition: When a Large or smaller creature that enters the trap’s space

Immovable Rod.name: Immovable Rod
Immovable Rod.description: This flat iron rod has a button on one end. You can use an action to press the button, which causes the rod to become magically fixed in place. Until you or another creature uses an action to push the button again, the rod doesn’t move, even if it defies gravity. The rod can hold up to 8,000 pounds of weight. More weight causes the rod to deactivate and fall. A creature can use an action to make a [[/check strength 30]] check, moving a fixed rod up to 10 feet on a success.

Instant Fortress.name: Instant Fortress
Instant Fortress.description:
You can use an action to place this 1-inch metal cube on the ground and speak its command word. The cube rapidly grows into a fortress that remains until you use an action to dismiss it, which works only if the fortress is empty.

The fortress is a square tower, 20 feet on a side and 30 feet high, with arrow slits on all sides and a battlement atop it. Its interior is divided into two floors, with a ladder running along one wall to connect them. The ladder ends at a trapdoor leading to the roof. When activated, the tower has a small door on the side facing you. The door opens only at your command, which you can speak as a bonus action. It is immune to the knock spell and similar magic, such as that of a chime of opening.

Each creature in the area where the fortress appears must make a [[/save]], taking [[/damage]] on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. In either case, the creature is pushed to an unoccupied space outside but next to the fortress. Objects in the area that aren’t being worn or carried take this damage and are pushed automatically.

The tower is made of adamantine, and its magic prevents it from being tipped over. The roof, the door, and the walls each have 100 HP, immunity to damage from nonmagical weapons excluding siege weapons, and resistance to all other damage. Only a wish spell can repair the fortress. Each casting of wish causes the roof, the door, or one wall to regain 50 HP.

Ioun Stone of Absorption.name: Ioun Stone of Absorption
Ioun Stone of Absorption.description:
While this pale lavender ellipsoid orbits your head, you can use your reaction to cancel a spell of 4th circle or lower cast by a creature you can see and targeting only you.

Once the stone has canceled 20 circles worth of spells, it burns out and turns dull gray, losing its magic. If you are targeted by a spell whose circle is higher than the number of spell rings the stone has left, the stone can’t cancel it.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Agility.name: Ioun Stone of Agility
Ioun Stone of Agility.description:
Your DEX score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20, while this deep red sphere orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Awareness.name: Ioun Stone of Awareness
Ioun Stone of Awareness.description:
You can’t be surprised while this dark blue rhomboid orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Fortitude.name: Ioun Stone of Fortitude
Ioun Stone of Fortitude.description:
Your CON score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20, while this pink rhomboid orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Greater Absorption.name: Ioun Stone of Greater Absorption
Ioun Stone of Greater Absorption.description:
While this marbled lavender and green ellipsoid orbits your head, you can use your reaction to cancel a spell of 8th circle or lower cast by a creature you can see and targeting only you.

Once the stone has canceled 50 circles worth of spells, it burns out and turns dull gray, losing its magic. If you are targeted by a spell whose circle is higher than the number of spell rings the stone has left, the stone can’t cancel it.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Insight.name: Ioun Stone of Insight
Ioun Stone of Insight.description:
Your WIS score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20, while this incandescent blue sphere orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Intellect.name: Ioun Stone of Intellect
Ioun Stone of Intellect.description:
Your INT score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20, while this marbled scarlet and blue sphere orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Leadership.name: Ioun Stone of Leadership
Ioun Stone of Leadership.description:
Your CHA score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20, while this marbled pink and green sphere orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Mastery.name: Ioun Stone of Mastery
Ioun Stone of Mastery.description:
Your proficiency bonus increases by 1 while this pale green prism orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Protection.name: Ioun Stone of Protection
Ioun Stone of Protection.description:
You gain a +1 bonus to AC while this dusty rose prism orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Regeneration.name: Ioun Stone of Regeneration
Ioun Stone of Regeneration.description:
You regain 15 HP at the end of each hour this pearly white spindle orbits your head, provided that you have at least 1 HP.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Regenerate.name: Regenerate
Regenerate.condition: At the end of each hour while you have at least 1 HP
Regenerate.activationCondition: At the end of each hour while you have at least 1 HP

Ioun Stone of Reserve.name: Ioun Stone of Reserve
Ioun Stone of Reserve.description:
This vibrant purple prism stores spells cast into it, holding them until you use them. The stone can store up to 3 circles worth of spells at a time. When found, it contains [[/r 1d4 - 1]] circles of stored spells chosen by the GM.

Any creature can cast a spell of 1st through 3rd circle into the stone by touching it as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect, other than to be stored in the stone. If the stone can’t hold the spell, the spell is expended without effect. The circle of the slot used to cast the spell determines how much space it uses.

While this stone orbits your head, you can cast any spell stored in it. The spell uses the spell slot, spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell. Once cast, the spell is expended, freeing up space.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Strength.name: Ioun Stone of Strength
Ioun Stone of Strength.description:
Your STR score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20, while this pale blue rhomboid orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Ioun Stone of Sustenance.name: Ioun Stone of Sustenance
Ioun Stone of Sustenance.description:
You don’t need to eat or drink while this clear spindle orbits your head.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.zHvTHITijHvb07FK.JournalEntryPage.f4AgDaCgKtvuDpSq inline]

Iron Bands of Binding.name: Iron Bands of Binding
Iron Bands of Binding.description:
This rusty iron sphere measures 3 inches in diameter and weighs 1 pound. You can use an action to speak the command word and throw the sphere at a Huge or smaller creature you can see within 60 feet of you. As the sphere moves through the air, it opens into a tangle of metal bands.

Make a ranged attack roll with an attack bonus equal to your DEX modifier plus your proficiency bonus. On a hit, the target is restrained until you take a bonus action to speak the command word to release it. Doing so, or missing with the attack, causes the bands to contract and become a sphere once more.

A creature, including the one restrained, can use an action to make a [[/check 20 STR]] to break the iron bands. On a success, the item is destroyed, and the restrained creature is freed. If the check fails, any further attempts made by that creature automatically fail until 24 hours have elapsed.

Once the bands are used, they can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Iron Flask.name: Iron Flask
Iron Flask.description:
This iron bottle has a brass stopper. You can use an action to speak the flask’s command word, targeting a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. If the target is native to a plane of existence other than the one you’re on, the target must succeed on a [[/save 17 WIS]] or be trapped in the flask. If the target has been trapped by the flask before, it has advantage on the save. Once trapped, a creature remains in the flask until released. The flask can hold only one creature at a time. A creature trapped in the flask doesn’t need to breathe, eat, or drink and doesn’t age.

You can use an action to remove the flask’s stopper and release the creature the flask contains. The creature is friendly to you and your companions for 1 hour and obeys your commands for that duration. If you give no commands or give it a command that is likely to result in its death, it defends itself but otherwise takes no actions. At the end of the duration, the creature acts in accordance with its normal disposition.

An identify spell reveals that a creature is inside the flask, but the only way to determine the type of creature is to open the flask. A newly discovered bottle might already contain a creature chosen by the GM or determined randomly. The GM has the creature’s statistics.

Javelin of Lightning.name: Javelin of Lightning
Javelin of Lightning.description:
This javelin is a magic weapon. When you hurl it and speak its command word, it transforms into a bolt of lightning, forming a line 5 feet wide that extends from you to a target within 120 feet. Each creature in the line excluding you and the target must make a [[/save 13 DEX]], taking [[/damage 4d6 lightning]] on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. The lightning bolt turns back into a javelin when it reaches the target. Make a ranged weapon attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes damage from the javelin plus [[/damage 4d6 lightning]].

The javelin’s property can’t be used again until the next dawn. In the meantime, the javelin can still be used as a magic weapon.

Lamp.name: Lamp
Lamp.description: A lamp casts bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Once lit, it burns for 6 hours on a flask (1 pint) of oil.

Lantern of Revealing.name: Lantern of Revealing
Lantern of Revealing.description: While lit, this hooded lantern burns for 6 hours on 1 pint of oil, shedding bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Invisible creatures and objects are visible if they are in the lantern’s bright light. You can use an action to lower the hood, reducing the light to dim light in a 5-foot radius.

Last Gasp.name: Last Gasp
Last Gasp.description: A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a [[/save]] save or be poisoned for 1 minute. The poisoned creature is paralyzed. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending both effects on a success.

Leather.name: Leather
Leather.description: The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.

Lock.name: Lock
Lock.description: A key is provided with the lock. Without the key, a creature proficient with thieves’ tools can pick this lock with a successful [[/tool dexterity thievesTools 15]] check. Your GM may decide that better locks are available for higher prices.

Longsword.name: Longsword
Longsword.description: [[/attack extended]]. [[/damage extended]], or [[/damage versatile average]] damage if used with two hands.

Luck Blade.name: Luck Blade
Luck Blade.description:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While carried, you also gain a +1 bonus to saves.

Luck. While you carry this sword, you can call on its luck (no action required) to reroll one attack roll, ability check, or save you dislike. You must use the second roll. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Wish. The sword has [[/r 1d4 - 1]] charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and cast the wish spell from it. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn and never recharges. The sword loses this property if it has no charges.

Foundry Note

Weapon (Any Sword), Legendary, Priceless (Requires Attunement)

Create the final magic item by selection the appropriate mundane item and dragging an enchantment from the Advancement & Effects tab to that item.

Luck.name: Luck
Luck.description: While you carry this sword, you can call on its luck (no action required) to reroll one attack roll, ability check, or save you dislike. You must use the second roll. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Wish.name: Wish
Wish.description: The sword has [[/r 1d4 − 1]] charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and cast the wish spell from it. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn and never recharges. The sword loses this property if it has no charges.

Lute.name: Lute
Lute.description:
Musical instruments come in many shapes and sizes with varying regional and cultural popularity. When you have proficiency in a musical instrument, choose one type: strings, brass, woodwinds, percussion, or keyboard instruments. You must have multiple proficiencies to have proficiency with more than one type of instrument.

Associated Abilities: DEX, CHA.

Components. A musical instrument typically includes an instrument, a protective case, and any small pieces necessary for play, such as picks or reeds.

Lyre.name: Lyre
Lyre.description:
Musical instruments come in many shapes and sizes with varying regional and cultural popularity. When you have proficiency in a musical instrument, choose one type: strings, brass, woodwinds, percussion, or keyboard instruments. You must have multiple proficiencies to have proficiency with more than one type of instrument.

Associated Abilities: DEX, CHA.

Components. A musical instrument typically includes an instrument, a protective case, and any small pieces necessary for play, such as picks or reeds.

Mace of Disruption.name: Mace of Disruption
Mace of Disruption.description:
When you hit a Fiend or Undead with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra [[/damage 2d6 radiant]]. If the target has 25 HP or fewer after taking this damage, it must succeed on a [[/save 15 WIS]] or be destroyed. On a successful save, the creature becomes &Reference[frightened] of you until the end of your next turn.

While you hold this weapon, it sheds bright light in a 20- foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

save.condition: When you hit a Fiend or Undead
save.activationCondition: When you hit a Fiend or Undead

Mace of Smiting.name: Mace of Smiting
Mace of Smiting.description:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The bonus increases to +3 when you use the mace to attack a Construct.

When you roll a 20 on an attack roll with this weapon, the target takes an extra [[/damage 2d6 bludgeoning]], or [[/damage 4d6 bludgeoning]] if it’s a Construct. If a Construct has 25 HP or fewer after taking this damage, it is destroyed.

Mace of Terror.name: Mace of Terror
Mace of Terror.description:
This magic weapon has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to release a wave of terror. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot radius extending from you must succeed on a [[/save 15 WIS]] or become &Reference[frightened] of you for 1 minute. While it is frightened in this way, a creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can’t willingly move to a space within 30 feet of you. It also can’t take reactions. For its action, it can use only the Dash action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If it has nowhere it can move, the creature can use the Dodge action. At the end of each of its turns, a creature can repeat the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.

The mace regains [[/r 1d3]] expended charges daily at dawn.

Terror.name: Terror
Terror.description: While you are frightened in this way, you must spend your turns trying to move as far away from the attacker as it can, and you can’t willingly move to a space within 30 feet of the attack. You also can’t take reactions. For its action, you can use only the Dash action or try to escape from an effect that prevents you from moving. If you have nowhere you can move, you can use the Dodge action. At the end of each of your turns, you can repeat the save, ending the effect on yourself on a success.

Magnifying Glass.name: Magnifying Glass
Magnifying Glass.description: This lens allows a closer look at small items. It is also useful as a substitute for flint and steel when starting fires. Lighting a fire with a magnifying glass requires sunlight to focus, tinder to light, and about 5 minutes for the fire to ignite. A magnifying glass grants advantage on any ability check made to appraise or inspect an item that is small or highly detailed.

Manacles.name: Manacles
Manacles.description: These metal restraints can bind a Small or Medium creature. To escape the manacles, a creature must use their action to make a successful [[/check dexterity 20]] check or break them with a successful [[/check strength 20]] check. Each set of manacles comes with one key. Without the key, a creature proficient with thieves’ tools can pick the manacles’ lock with a successful [[/check dexterity 15]] check. Manacles are an object with AC 19 and 15 hit points.

Mantle of Spell Resistance.name: Mantle of Spell Resistance
Mantle of Spell Resistance.description: You have advantage on saves against spells while you wear this cloak.

Manual of Bodily Health.name: Manual of Bodily Health
Manual of Bodily Health.description: This handwritten book contains exercises and insights into improving your ability scores. Their words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying a book’s contents and practicing its guidelines, your CON score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. Afterward, the manual loses its magic, but regains it in a century.

Manual of Gainful Exercise.name: Manual of Gainful Exercise
Manual of Gainful Exercise.description: This handwritten book contains exercises and insights into improving your ability scores. Their words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying a book’s contents and practicing its guidelines, your STR score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. Afterward, the manual loses its magic, but regains it in a century.

Manual of Golems, Clay.name: Manual of Golems, Clay
Manual of Golems, Clay.description:
This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a particular type of creature with the Golem tag. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-circle spell slots. A creature that can’t use a manual of golems and attempts to read it takes [[/damage 6d6 psychic]].

To create a golem, you must spend 30 days, working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay the specified cost to purchase supplies.

Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands.

Manual of Golems, Flesh.name: Manual of Golems, Flesh
Manual of Golems, Flesh.description:
This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a particular type of creature with the Golem tag. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-circle spell slots. A creature that can’t use a manual of golems and attempts to read it takes [[/damage 6d6 psychic]].

To create a golem, you must spend 60 days, working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay the specified cost to purchase supplies.

Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands.

Manual of Golems, Iron.name: Manual of Golems, Iron
Manual of Golems, Iron.description:
This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a particular type of creature with the Golem tag. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-circle spell slots. A creature that can’t use a manual of golems and attempts to read it takes [[/damage 6d6 psychic]].

To create a golem, you must spend 120 days, working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay the specified cost to purchase supplies.

Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands.

Manual of Golems, Stone.name: Manual of Golems, Stone
Manual of Golems, Stone.description:
This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a particular type of creature with the Golem tag. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-circle spell slots. A creature that can’t use a manual of golems and attempts to read it takes [[/damage 6d6 psychic]].

To create a golem, you must spend 90 days, working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay the specified cost to purchase supplies.

Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands.

Manual of Quickness of Action.name: Manual of Quickness of Action
Manual of Quickness of Action.description: This handwritten book contains exercises and insights into improving your ability scores. Their words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying a book’s contents and practicing its guidelines, your DEX score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. Afterward, the manual loses its magic, but regains it in a century.

Map Case.name: Map Case
Map Case.description: This cylindrical leather case can hold up to ten rolled-up sheets of paper.

Marvelous Pigments.name: Marvelous Pigments
Marvelous Pigments.description:
Typically found in [[/r 1d4]] pots inside a fine wooden box with a brush (weighing 1 pound in total), these pigments allow you to create three-dimensional objects by painting them in two dimensions. The paint flows from the brush to form the desired object as you concentrate on its image.

Each pot of paint is sufficient to cover 1,000 square feet of a surface, which lets you create inanimate objects or terrain features—such as a door, a pit, flowers, trees, cells, rooms, or weapons—that are up to 10,000 cubic feet. It takes 10 minutes to cover 100 square feet.

When you complete the painting, the object or terrain feature depicted becomes a real, nonmagical object. Thus, painting a door on a wall creates an actual door that can be opened to whatever is beyond. Painting a pit on a floor creates a real pit, and its depth counts against the total area of objects you create.

Nothing created by the pigments can have a value greater than 25 gp. If you paint an object of greater value (such as a diamond or a pile of gold), the object looks authentic, but close inspection reveals it is made from paste, bone, or some other worthless material.

If you paint a form of energy such as fire or lightning, the energy appears but dissipates as soon as you complete the painting, doing no harm.

Medallion of Thoughts.name: Medallion of Thoughts
Medallion of Thoughts.description: This medallion has 3 charges. While wearing it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.q3ggx5aH69iqrcL3]{detect thoughts} spell (save DC 13) from it. The medallion regains [[/r 1d3]] expended charges daily at dawn.

Merchant’s Scale.name: Merchant’s Scale
Merchant’s Scale.description: A scale includes a small balance, pans, and a suitable assortment of weights up to 2 lb. With it, you can measure the exact weight of suitably sized objects, such as raw precious metals or trade goods, to help determine their worth.

Mess Kit.name: Mess Kit
Mess Kit.description: This tin box contains a cup and simple cutlery. The box clamps together, and one side can be used as a cooking pan and the other as a plate or shallow bowl.

Midnight Tears.name: Midnight Tears
Midnight Tears.description: A creature who ingests this poison suffers no effect until the stroke of midnight. If the poison has not been neutralized before then, the creature must succeed on a [[/save]] save, taking [[/damage average]] damage on a failure or half as much damage on a success.

Navigator Tools.name: Navigator Tools
Navigator Tools.description:
Navigator tools are used to read and draw maps, find your path or prevent you from becoming lost, and determine likely locations on a map for secret doors or hidden features.

Associated Abilities: INT, WIS.

Components. This tool kit includes quills, ink, and parchment along with two compasses, calipers, and a ruler as well as a sextant or telescope.

Draw Map. Navigator tools can be used to create accurate maps while traveling, which grant advantage on checks made to prevent becoming lost.

Necklace of Adaptation.name: Necklace of Adaptation
Necklace of Adaptation.description: While wearing this necklace, you can breathe normally in any environment. You also have advantage on saves made against harmful gases and vapors, such as cloudkill and stinking cloud effects, inhaled poisons, and the breath weapons of some dragons.

Necklace of Fireballs.name: Necklace of Fireballs
Necklace of Fireballs.description:
per bead This necklace has [[/r 1d6 + 3]] beads hanging from it. You can use an action to detach a bead and throw it up to 60 feet away. When it reaches the end of its trajectory, the bead detonates as a 3rd-circle @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.VYvD59yJYefhAwOI]{fireball} spell (save DC 15).

You can hurl multiple beads, or even the whole necklace, as one action. When you do so, increase the spell circle of the fireball by 1 for each bead beyond the first.

Necklace of Prayer Beads.name: Necklace of Prayer Beads
Necklace of Prayer Beads.description:
This necklace has [[/r 1d4 + 2]] magic beads made from aquamarine, black pearl, or topaz. It also has nonmagical beads made from stones such as amber, bloodstone, citrine, coral, jade, pearl, or quartz. If a magic bead is removed from the necklace, that bead loses its magic.

Six types of magic beads exist. The GM decides the type of each bead on the necklace or determines it randomly. A necklace can have more than one bead of the same type. To use one, you must be wearing the necklace. Each bead contains a spell that you can cast from it as a bonus action (using your spell save DC if necessary). Once a bead’s spell is cast, that bead can’t be used again until the next dawn.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.PBo9j135EucIDliX.JournalEntryPage.rDMWnsy2GdAxQElp inline]

Foundry Note

Each bead tracks its quantity using the uses on the activity except the Bead of Curing, which tracks it using the item uses. Adjust the various maximum uses to match the beads found and remove an unnecessary activities.

Net.name: Net
Net.description: As an action, you can throw a net at a Large or smaller creature within 15 feet of you. The net has no effect on creatures that are formless or incorporeal (like ghosts). Make a ranged attack against the creature, treating the net as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target is restrained until it is freed. A creature can use its action to make a [[/check strength athletics 10]] check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) also frees the creature without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the net.

Nine Lives Stealer.name: Nine Lives Stealer
Nine Lives Stealer.description:
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The sword has [[/r 1d8 + 1]] charges. If you score a critical hit against a creature that has fewer than 100 HP, it must succeed on a [[/save 15 CON]] or be slain instantly as the sword tears its life force from its body (a Construct or an Undead is immune). The sword loses 1 charge if the creature is slain. When the sword has no charges remaining, it loses this property.

Foundry Note

Weapon (Any Sword), Very Rare, 8,800 gp (Requires Attunement)

Create the final magic item by selection the appropriate mundane item and dragging an enchantment from the Advancement & Effects tab to that item.

save.condition: When you score a critical hit against a creature that has fewer than 100 HP
save.activationCondition: When you score a critical hit against a creature that has fewer than 100 HP

Oathbow.name: Oathbow
Oathbow.description:
When you nock an arrow on this bow, it whispers in Elvish, Swift defeat to my enemies. When you use this weapon to make a ranged attack, you can, as a command phrase, say, Swift death to you who have wronged me. The target of your attack becomes your sworn enemy until it dies or until dawn seven days later. You can have only one such sworn enemy at a time. When your sworn enemy dies, you can choose a new one after the next dawn.

When you make a ranged attack roll with this weapon against your sworn enemy, you have advantage on the roll. In addition, your target gains no benefit from cover, other than total cover, and you suffer no disadvantage due to long range. If the attack hits, your sworn enemy takes an extra [[/damage 3d6 piercing]].

While your sworn enemy lives, you have disadvantage on attack rolls with all other weapons.

Sworn Enemy Damage.name: Sworn Enemy Damage
Sworn Enemy Damage.condition: When you hit your sworn enemy
Sworn Enemy Damage.activationCondition: When you hit your sworn enemy

Oil (flask).name: Oil (flask)
Oil (flask).description: Oil usually comes in a clay flask that holds 1 pint. As an action, you can splash the oil in this flask onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw it up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a target creature or object, treating the oil as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target is covered in oil. If the target takes any fire damage before the oil dries (after 1 minute), the target takes an additional 5 fire damage from the burning oil. You can also pour a flask of oil on the ground to cover a 5-foot-square area, provided that the surface is level. If lit, the oil burns for 2 rounds and deals 5 fire damage to any creature that enters the area or ends its turn in the area. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

Oil of Etherealness.name: Oil of Etherealness
Oil of Etherealness.description: Beads of this cloudy gray oil form on the outside of its container and quickly evaporate. The oil can cover a Medium or smaller creature, along with the equipment it’s wearing and carrying (one additional vial is required for each size category above Medium). Applying the oil takes 10 minutes. The affected creature then gains the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.oTs4HWDpPWNNEdqv]{etherealness} spell for 1 hour.

Oil of Sharpness.name: Oil of Sharpness
Oil of Sharpness.description: This clear, gelatinous oil sparkles with tiny, ultrathin silver shards. The oil can coat one slashing or piercing weapon or up to 5 pieces of slashing or piercing ammunition. Applying the oil takes 1 minute. For 1 hour, the coated item is magical and has a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

Oil of Slipperiness.name: Oil of Slipperiness
Oil of Slipperiness.description:
This sticky, black unguent is thick and heavy in the container, but it flows quickly when poured. The oil can cover a Medium or smaller creature, along with the equipment it wears and carries (one additional vial is required for each size category above Medium). Applying the oil takes 10 minutes. The affected creature then gains the effect of a @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.ebGqTLYBFxSDf3g7]{freedom of movement} spell for 8 hours.

Alternatively, the oil can be poured on the ground as an action, where it covers a 10-foot square, duplicating the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.vj70K3TUp67pl6ta]{grease} spell in that area for 8 hours.

Padded.name: Padded
Padded.description: This full-body outfit consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.

Pearl of Power.name: Pearl of Power
Pearl of Power.description: While this pearl is on your person, you can use an action to speak its command word and regain one expended spell slot. If the expended slot was of 4th circle or higher, the new slot is 3rd circle. Once you use the pearl, it can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Periapt of Health.name: Periapt of Health
Periapt of Health.description: You are immune to contracting any disease while you wear this pendant. If you are already infected with a disease, the effects are suppressed while you wear the pendant.

Periapt of Proof against Poison.name: Periapt of Proof against Poison
Periapt of Proof against Poison.description: This delicate silver chain has a brilliant-cut black gem pendant. While you wear it, poisons have no effect on you. You are immune to the poisoned condition and have immunity to poison damage.

Proof against Poison.name: Proof against Poison
Proof against Poison.description: You are immune to the poisoned condition and have immunity to poison damage.

Periapt of Wound Closure.name: Periapt of Wound Closure
Periapt of Wound Closure.description: While you wear this pendant, you stabilize whenever you are dying at the start of your turn. In addition, when you roll a hit die to regain hit points, double the number of hit points it restores.

Pipes of Haunting.name: Pipes of Haunting
Pipes of Haunting.description: You must have proficiency with instruments (woodwinds) to use these pipes. They have 3 charges. You can use an action to play them and expend 1 charge to create an eerie, spellbinding tune. Each creature within 30 feet of you that hears you play must succeed on a [[/save 15 WIS]] or become frightened of you for 1 minute. If you wish, all creatures in the area that aren’t hostile toward you automatically succeed on the save. A creature that fails the save can repeat it at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. A creature that succeeds on its save is immune to the effect of these pipes for 24 hours. The pipes regain [[/r 1d3]] expended charges daily at dawn.

Plate.name: Plate
Plate.description: Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.

Portable Hole.name: Portable Hole
Portable Hole.description:
This fine black cloth, soft as silk, is folded up to the dimensions of a handkerchief. It unfolds into a circular sheet 6 feet in diameter.

You can use an action to unfold a portable hole and place it on or against a solid surface, whereupon the portable hole creates an extradimensional hole 10 feet deep. The cylindrical space within the hole exists on a different plane, so it can’t be used to create open passages. Any creature inside an open portable hole can exit the hole by climbing out of it.

You can use an action to close a portable hole by taking hold of the edges of the cloth and folding it up. Folding the cloth closes the hole, and any creatures or objects within remain in the extradimensional space. No matter what’s in it, the hole weighs next to nothing.

If the hole is folded up, a creature within the hole’s extradimensional space can use an action to make a [[/check 10 STR]]. On a successful check, the creature forces its way out and appears within 5 feet of the portable hole or the creature carrying it. A breathing creature within a closed portable hole can survive for up to 10 minutes, after which time it begins to suffocate.

Placing a portable hole inside an extradimensional space created by a bag of holding, handy haversack, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate is sucked through it and deposited in a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can’t be reopened.

Portable Ram.name: Portable Ram
Portable Ram.description: You can use a portable ram to break down doors. You have advantage on relevant STR (Athletic) checks made to do so.

Potion of Acid Resistance.name: Potion of Acid Resistance
Potion of Acid Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to acid damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Animal Friendship.name: Potion of Animal Friendship
Potion of Animal Friendship.description: When you drink this potion, animals tend to be friendly to you for 1 hour. For the duration, you can use an action to cast the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.F1s8TRosgOUvR4iv]{animal friendship} spell (save DC 13) at any time. Agitating this muddy liquid brings little bits into view: a fish scale, a hummingbird tongue, a cat claw, or a squirrel hair.

Potion of Clairvoyance.name: Potion of Clairvoyance
Potion of Clairvoyance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.0DXCIjbQmk9UFSzp]{clairvoyance} spell. An eyeball bobs in this yellowish liquid but vanishes when the potion is opened.

Potion of Climbing.name: Potion of Climbing
Potion of Climbing.description: When you drink this potion, you gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed for 1 hour. During this time, you have advantage on STR (Athletics) checks you make to climb. The potion is separated into brown, silver, and gray layers resembling bands of stone.

Potion of Cloud Giant Strength.name: Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
Potion of Cloud Giant Strength.description:
When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 27 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

This potion’s transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a cloud giant.

Cloud Giant Strength.name: Cloud Giant Strength
Cloud Giant Strength.description: When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 27 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

Potion of Cold Resistance.name: Potion of Cold Resistance
Potion of Cold Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to cold damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Diminution.name: Potion of Diminution
Potion of Diminution.description: When you drink this potion, you gain the “reduce” effect of the enlarge/reduce spell for [[/r 1d4]] hours (no concentration required). The red in the potion’s liquid continuously contracts to a tiny bead and then expands to color the clear liquid around it.

Potion of Due Notice.name: Potion of Due Notice
Potion of Due Notice.description: When you drink this potion, double your PB for the purpose of calculating your passive Insight, Investigation, and Perception scores for 1 hour. If you don't have proficiency in any of these skills, you gain proficiency in them for the duration of the potion. This potion’s liquid has a bright green effervescence.

Potion of Fire Giant Strength.name: Potion of Fire Giant Strength
Potion of Fire Giant Strength.description:
When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 25 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

This potion’s transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a fire giant.

Fire Giant Strength.name: Fire Giant Strength
Fire Giant Strength.description: When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 25 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

Potion of Fire Resistance.name: Potion of Fire Resistance
Potion of Fire Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to fire damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Flying.name: Potion of Flying
Potion of Flying.description: When you drink this potion, you gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 hour and can hover. If you’re in the air when the potion wears off, you fall unless you have some other means of staying aloft. This potion’s clear liquid floats at the top of its container and cloudy white impurities drift in it.

Potion of Force Resistance.name: Potion of Force Resistance
Potion of Force Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to force damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Frost Giant Strength.name: Potion of Frost Giant Strength
Potion of Frost Giant Strength.description:
When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 23 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

This potion’s transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a frost giant.

Frost Giant Strength.name: Frost Giant Strength
Frost Giant Strength.description: When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 23 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

Potion of Gaseous Form.name: Potion of Gaseous Form
Potion of Gaseous Form.description: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.fUkZjWTGW0wFntSB]{gaseous form} spell for 1 hour (no concentration required) or until you end the effect as a bonus action. This potion’s container seems to hold fog that moves and pours like water.

Potion of Greater Healing.name: Potion of Greater Healing
Potion of Greater Healing.description: This red, glimmering potion allows you to regain hit points when you drink it. This potion recovers [[/r 4d4 + 4]] hit points.

healing.description: You regain hit points when you drink this red, glimmering potion.

Potion of Growth.name: Potion of Growth
Potion of Growth.description: When you drink this potion, you gain the enlarge effect of the enlarge/reduce spell for [[/r 1d4]] hours (no concentration required). The red in the potion’s liquid continuously expands from a tiny bead to color the clear liquid around it and then contracts. Shaking the bottle fails to interrupt this process.

Potion of Healing.name: Potion of Healing
Potion of Healing.description: This red, glimmering potion allows you to regain hit points when you drink it. This potion recovers [[/r 2d4 + 2]] hit points.

healing.description: You regain hit points when you drink this red, glimmering potion.

Potion of Heroism.name: Potion of Heroism
Potion of Heroism.description: For 1 hour after drinking it, you gain 10 temporary HP that last for 1 hour. For the same duration, you are under the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.Gnxh5w9pE08ZGF9S]{bless} spell (no concentration required). This blue potion bubbles and steams as if boiling.

Potion of Hill Giant Strength.name: Potion of Hill Giant Strength
Potion of Hill Giant Strength.description:
When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 21 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

This potion’s transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a hill giant.

Hill Giant Strength.name: Hill Giant Strength
Hill Giant Strength.description: When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 21 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

Potion of Invisibility.name: Potion of Invisibility
Potion of Invisibility.description: When you drink this potion, you become &Reference[invisible] for 1 hour. Anything you wear or carry is invisible with you. The effect ends early if you attack or cast a spell. This potion’s container looks empty but feels as though it holds liquid.

Potion of Lightning Resistance.name: Potion of Lightning Resistance
Potion of Lightning Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to lightning damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Mind Reading.name: Potion of Mind Reading
Potion of Mind Reading.description: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.q3ggx5aH69iqrcL3]{detect thoughts} spell (save DC 13). The potion’s dense, purple liquid has an ovoid cloud of pink floating in it.

Potion of Necrotic Resistance.name: Potion of Necrotic Resistance
Potion of Necrotic Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to necrotic damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Poison Resistance.name: Potion of Poison Resistance
Potion of Poison Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to poison damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Psychic Resistance.name: Potion of Psychic Resistance
Potion of Psychic Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to psychic damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Radiant Resistance.name: Potion of Radiant Resistance
Potion of Radiant Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to radiant damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Speed.name: Potion of Speed
Potion of Speed.description: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.aHifQ78fmPqA4oAJ]{haste} spell for 1 minute (no concentration required). The potion’s yellow fluid is streaked with black and swirls on its own.

Potion of Stone Giant Strength.name: Potion of Stone Giant Strength
Potion of Stone Giant Strength.description:
When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 23 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

This potion’s transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a stone giant.

Stone Giant Strength.name: Stone Giant Strength
Stone Giant Strength.description: When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 23 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

Potion of Storm Giant Strength.name: Potion of Storm Giant Strength
Potion of Storm Giant Strength.description:
When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 29 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

This potion’s transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a storm giant.

Storm Giant Strength.name: Storm Giant Strength
Storm Giant Strength.description: When you drink this potion, your STR score changes to 29 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your STR score equals or exceeds that score.

Potion of Superior Healing.name: Potion of Superior Healing
Potion of Superior Healing.description: This red, glimmering potion allows you to regain hit points when you drink it. This potion recovers [[/r 8d4 + 8]] hit points.

healing.description: You regain hit points when you drink this red, glimmering potion.

Potion of Supreme Healing.name: Potion of Supreme Healing
Potion of Supreme Healing.description: This red, glimmering potion allows you to regain hit points when you drink it. This potion recovers [[/r 10d4 + 20]] hit points.

healing.description: You regain hit points when you drink this red, glimmering potion.

Potion of Thunder Resistance.name: Potion of Thunder Resistance
Potion of Thunder Resistance.description: When you drink this potion, you gain resistance to thunder damage for 1 hour.

Potion of Water Breathing.name: Potion of Water Breathing
Potion of Water Breathing.description: You can breathe underwater for 1 hour after drinking this potion. Its cloudy green fluid smells of the sea and has a jellyfish-like bubble floating in it.

Pouch.name: Pouch
Pouch.description: A cloth or leather pouch can hold up to 20 sling bullets or 50 blowgun needles, among other things. A compartmentalized pouch for holding spell components is called a @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.items.Item.f32H3HCS3Gg19diW]{component pouch}.

Priest's Pack.name: Priest's Pack
Priest's Pack.description: Priest’s Pack (19 gp). Includes a backpack, a blanket, 10 candles, a tinderbox, an alms box, 2 blocks of incense, a censer, vestments, 2 days of rations, and a waterskin.

Primordial Focus.name: Primordial Focus
Primordial Focus.description: This item can be used as a spellcasting focus to channel Primordial spells. Examples include a totem made from natural materials like feather or bone, a wooden staff carved from a living tree, or a yew wand.

Provisioner Tools.name: Provisioner Tools
Provisioner Tools.description:
Provisioner tools are used to prepare meals, make rations, and craft beverages.

Associated Abilities: CON, WIS.

Components. This tool kit includes a large jug, several jars, a siphon with several feet of tubing, a rolled set of knives for chopping, sawing, paring, and deboning, a metal soup pot and a large skillet, a small bundle of spoons, spatulas, ladels, and whisks, several mixing bowls, a cutting board, and quantities of hops, dried herbs, and powdered spices.

Craft Items. Provisioner tools can be used to make items with the crafting downtime activity.

Quarterstaff.name: Quarterstaff
Quarterstaff.description: [[/attack extended]]. [[/damage extended]], or [[/damage versatile average]] damage if used with two hands.

Quiver.name: Quiver
Quiver.description: A quiver can hold up to 20 arrows or 20 crossbow bolts.

Rations (1 day).name: Rations (1 day)
Rations (1 day).description: Rations consist of dry foods suitable for extended travel, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. One unit of rations is enough to feed one Medium or smaller creature for one day.

Restorative Ointment.name: Restorative Ointment
Restorative Ointment.description:
This glass jar, 3 inches in diameter, contains [[/r 1d4 + 1]] doses of a thick mixture that smells faintly of aloe. The jar and its contents weigh 1/2 pound.

As an action, one dose of the ointment can be swallowed or applied to the skin. The creature that receives it regains [[/healing 2d8 + 2]]{2d8 + 2} hit points, ceases to be poisoned, and is cured of any disease.

Riding Saddle.name: Riding Saddle
Riding Saddle.description: A riding saddle braces the rider, helping you keep your seat on an active mount in battle. It gives you advantage on any check you make to remain mounted. An exotic saddle is required for riding any aquatic or flying mount.

Ring Mail.name: Ring Mail
Ring Mail.description: This leather armor has heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against attacks.

Ring of Acid Resistance.name: Ring of Acid Resistance
Ring of Acid Resistance.description: You have resistance to acid damage while wearing this ring. The pearl in the ring indicates the type.

Acid Resistance.name: Acid Resistance
Acid Resistance.description: You have resistance to acid damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Animal Influence.name: Ring of Animal Influence
Ring of Animal Influence.description:
This ring has 3 charges and regains [[/r 1d3]] expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the ring, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells:

  • @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.F1s8TRosgOUvR4iv]{Animal friendship} (save DC 13)

  • @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.moUAyN0JUuMvoY6U]{Fear} (save DC 13), targeting only Beasts or creatures with the Animal tag that have an INT score of 3 or lower

  • @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.VEYRZOPPA3yH6AeK]{Speak with animals}

cast.target: Beasts or creatures with the Animal tag that have an INT score of 3 or lower

Ring of Cold Resistance.name: Ring of Cold Resistance
Ring of Cold Resistance.description: You have resistance to cold damage while wearing this ring. The tourmaline in the ring indicates the type.

Cold Resistance.name: Cold Resistance
Cold Resistance.description: You have resistance to cold damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Djinni Summoning.name: Ring of Djinni Summoning
Ring of Djinni Summoning.description:
While wearing this ring, you can speak its command word as an action to summon a particular djinni from the Elemental Plane of Air. The djinni appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 120 feet of you. It remains as long as you concentrate (as if concentrating on a spell), for up to 1 hour or until it drops to 0 HP. It then returns to its home plane.

While summoned, the djinni is friendly to you and your companions. It obeys any commands you give it, no matter what language you use. If you fail to command it, the djinni defends itself against attackers but takes no other actions.

After the djinni departs, it can’t be summoned again for 24 hours. The ring becomes nonmagical if the djinni dies.

Ring of Evasion.name: Ring of Evasion
Ring of Evasion.description: This ring has 3 charges and regains [[/r 1d3]] expended charges daily at dawn. When you fail a DEX save while wearing it, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge to succeed on that save instead.

Succeed on Save.name: Succeed on Save
Succeed on Save.condition: When you fail a DEX save
Succeed on Save.activationCondition: When you fail a DEX save

Ring of Feather Falling.name: Ring of Feather Falling
Ring of Feather Falling.description: When you fall while wearing this ring, you descend 60 feet per round and take no damage from falling.

Ring of Fire Resistance.name: Ring of Fire Resistance
Ring of Fire Resistance.description: You have resistance to fire damage while wearing this ring. The garnet in the ring indicates the type.

Fire Resistance.name: Fire Resistance
Fire Resistance.description: You have resistance to fire damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Force Resistance.name: Ring of Force Resistance
Ring of Force Resistance.description: You have resistance to force damage while wearing this ring. The sapphire in the ring indicates the type.

Force Resistance.name: Force Resistance
Force Resistance.description: You have resistance to force damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Free Action.name: Ring of Free Action
Ring of Free Action.description: While you wear this ring, difficult terrain doesn’t cost you extra movement. In addition, magic can neither reduce your speed nor cause you to be paralyzed or restrained.

Free Action.name: Free Action
Free Action.description: Difficult terrain doesn’t cost you extra movement.

Ring of Invisibility.name: Ring of Invisibility
Ring of Invisibility.description: While wearing this ring, you can turn &Reference[invisible] as an action. Anything you are wearing or carrying is invisible with you. You remain invisible until the ring is removed, until you attack or cast a spell, or until you use a bonus action to become visible again.

Ring of Jumping.name: Ring of Jumping
Ring of Jumping.description: While wearing this ring, you can cast the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.V6dBngLQzGP0DrQt]{longstrider} spell from it as a bonus action. You can target only yourself with it.

Ring of Lightning Resistance.name: Ring of Lightning Resistance
Ring of Lightning Resistance.description: You have resistance to lightning damage while wearing this ring. The citrine in the ring indicates the type.

Lightning Resistance.name: Lightning Resistance
Lightning Resistance.description: You have resistance to lightning damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Mind Shielding.name: Ring of Mind Shielding
Ring of Mind Shielding.description:
While wearing this ring, you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, or know your creature type.

Creatures can telepathically communicate with you only if you allow it.

You can use an action to cause the ring to become invisible until you use another action to make it visible, until you remove the ring, or until you die.

If you die while wearing the ring, your soul enters it, unless it already houses a soul. You can remain in the ring or depart for the afterlife. As long as your soul is in the ring, you can telepathically communicate with any creature wearing it. A wearer can’t prevent this telepathic communication.

Ring of Necrotic Resistance.name: Ring of Necrotic Resistance
Ring of Necrotic Resistance.description: You have resistance to necrotic damage while wearing this ring. The jet in the ring indicates the type.

Necrotic Resistance.name: Necrotic Resistance
Necrotic Resistance.description: You have resistance to necrotic damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Poison Resistance.name: Ring of Poison Resistance
Ring of Poison Resistance.description: You have resistance to poison damage while wearing this ring. The amethyst in the ring indicates the type.

Poison Resistance.name: Poison Resistance
Poison Resistance.description: You have resistance to poison damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Protection.name: Ring of Protection
Ring of Protection.description: You gain a +1 bonus to AC and saves while wearing this ring.

Ring of Psychic Resistance.name: Ring of Psychic Resistance
Ring of Psychic Resistance.description: You have resistance to psychic damage while wearing this ring. The jade in the ring indicates the type.

Psychic Resistance.name: Psychic Resistance
Psychic Resistance.description: You have resistance to psychic damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Radiant Resistance.name: Ring of Radiant Resistance
Ring of Radiant Resistance.description: You have resistance to radiant damage while wearing this ring. The topaz in the ring indicates the type.

Radiant Resistance.name: Radiant Resistance
Radiant Resistance.description: You have resistance to radiant damage while wearing this ring.

Ring of Regeneration.name: Ring of Regeneration
Ring of Regeneration.description: While wearing this ring, you regain [[/healing 1d6]] every 10 minutes, provided that you have at least 1 HP. If you lose a body part, the ring causes the missing part to regrow and return to full functionality after [[/r 1d6 + 1]] days if you have at least 1 HP the whole time.

Ring of Shooting Stars.name: Ring of Shooting Stars
Ring of Shooting Stars.description:
While wearing this ring in dim light or darkness, as an action you can cast dancing lights and light from it. For its other properties, the ring has 6 charges.

Faerie Fire. You can expend 1 charge as an action to cast @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.6dvUhdyeuOtRySf9]{faerie fire} from the ring.

Ball Lightning. You can expend 2 charges as an action to create one to four 3-foot-diameter spheres of lightning. The more spheres you create, the less power each sphere has.

Each sphere appears in an unoccupied space you can see within 120 feet of you. The spheres last as long as you concentrate (as if concentrating on a spell), up to 1 minute. Each sphere sheds dim light in a 30-foot radius. As a bonus action, you can move each sphere up to 30 feet, but no farther than 120 feet away from you. When a creature other than you comes within 5 feet of a sphere, the sphere discharges lightning at that creature and disappears. That creature must make a [[/save 15 DEX]]. On a failed save, the creature takes lightning damage based on the number of spheres you created.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.PBo9j135EucIDliX.JournalEntryPage.Cnttuvo9mkg9K3jX inline]

Shooting Stars. You can expend 1 to 3 charges as an action. For every charge you expend, you launch a glowing mote of light from the ring at a point you can see within 60 feet of you. Each creature within a 15-foot cube originating from that point is showered in sparks and must make a [[/save 15 DEX]], taking [[/damage 5d4 fire]] on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

The ring regains [[/r 1d6]] expended charges daily at dawn.

Ring of Spell Storing.name: Ring of Spell Storing
Ring of Spell Storing.description:
This ring stores spells cast into it, holding them until the attuned wearer uses them. The ring can store up to 5 circles’ worth of spells at a time. When found, it contains [[/r 1d6 - 1]] circles of stored spells chosen by the GM.

Any creature can cast a spell of 1st through 5th circle into the ring by touching the ring as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect, other than to be stored in the ring. If the ring can’t hold the spell, the spell is expended without effect. The circle of the spell slot used to cast the spell determines how much space it uses.

While wearing this ring, you can cast any spell stored in it.
